AND ADD IN ACCORDANCE WITH C/P
IN CASE OF ECO SPEED AND CONSUMPTION AT REDUCED RPM, PROVIDING SAME DOES NOT INTERFERE WITH SAFE OPERATION OF THE VESSEL AND HER MACHINERY, VESSEL ON DAILY BASIS TO INCREASE RPM FOR MINIMUM 1 HOUR DURING WHICH VESSEL TO SAIL AT OPERATIONAL SPEED AND CONSUMPTION.
ALL SPEED/CONSUMPTIONS FIGURES ARE APPROXIMATE PER 24 CONSECUTIVE HOURS AND TO BE APPLIED ONLY TO VOYAGES EXCEEDING 48 HOURS TRANSIT TIME FROM SEA PILOT TO SEA PILOT PERFORMED DURING PERIODS OF GOOD WEATHER BEING CONDITIONS UP TO BEAUFORT SCALE 4 / DOUGLAS STATE OF SEA 3 SIGNIFICANT WAVE HEIGHT UP TO 3 TO 5 FEET, WITHOUT ADVERSE CURRENT OR SWELL AND EXCLUDING PASSAGE WITH RESTRICTED VISIBILITY AND PASSAGE IN CONGESTED WATERS. SPEED/CONSUMPTION FIGURES ARE NOT TO BE APPLIED TO AND ARE NOT EFFECTIVE IN RESPECT OF ANY PERIODS WHATSOEVER DURING WHICH THE WEATHER CONDITIONS EXCEED BEAUFORT SCALE 4 / DOUGLAS STATE OF SEA 3 SIGNIFICANT WAVE HEIGHT UP TO 3 TO 5 FEET WITH ADVERSE CURRENT, OR SWELL OR PASSAGE WITH RESTRICTED VISILBILITY OR IN CONGESTED WATERS . ABOUT IS UNDERSTOOD TO MEAN 0.5KNOTS DOWNWARDS IN THE SPEED AND 5% UPWARDS IN THE CONSUMPTION.
VESSEL HAS LIBERTY TO USE MGO WHEN MANEUVERING IN/OUT OF PORTS OR IN NARROW/SHALLOW WATERS, CANALS OR IN ROUGH SEA, IN RIVER, STARTING UP/TURNING OFF ENGINES, BALLAST/DEBALLAST, CLEANING VESSEL’S HOLD(S).
A. AVERAGE SPEED AND AVERAGE CONSUMPTION TO BE CALCULATED FROM SEA PILOT TO SEA PILOT EXCLUDING PERIODS OF SLOW STEAMING ON CHARTERERS’ ORDER OR FOR REASONS OF SAFETY OR STANDING BY ASSISTING ANOTHER VESSEL IN DISTRESS.

OWNERS WARRANT THE VESSEL IS CAPABLE OF MAINTAINING AND SHALL MAINTAIN THROUGHOUT THE PERIOD OF THE CHARTER PARTY, FROM BEGINNING SEA PASSAGE TO END SEA PASSAGE,
EXCLUDING ANY VOYAGE UP TO 36 HOURS DURATION, UP TO AND INCLUDING BEAUFORT SCALE 4 AND DOUGLAS SEA STATE 3, WITH NO ADVERSE CURRENTS, AND WITH COMBINED WAVE AND SWELL HEIGHTS NTE 1.25M,
BEING ON EVEN KEEL AND EXCLUDING PERIODS DURING REDUCTIONS OF SPEED FOR SAFETY, AVOIDING PIRACY THREATS, CONGESTION OR REDUCED VISIBILITY,NOT EXCEEDING SUMMER DRAFT, ETC. GOOD WEATHER PERIOD TO BE 24 HOURS CONSECUTIVE.
REFERRING TO VESSEL SPEED AND CONSUMPTION IT IS AGREED THAT \'ABOUT\' IS DEFINED AS 5% MORE ON BUNKER CONSUMPTION AND 0.5 KNOTS LESS ON VESSEL SPEED. ANY PERFORMANCE EVALUATION REPORT SUBMITTED TO OWNERS MUST REFLECT
ALL RELATED TERMS AGREED IN VESSEL\'S DESCRIPTION INCLUDING THE OFF-SETS AND ALLOWING FOR BOTH \'ABOUTS\'.
SERVICE SPEED:
LADEN : ABT 13.50 KTS ON ABT 23.00 MT VLSFO + 0.1MT MGO
BALLAST : ABT 14.00 KTS ON ABT 22.50 MT VLSFO + 0.1 MT MGO
IN PORT :
IDLE : ABT 3  MT/DAY IFO(380CST) +  ABT 0.1MT/DAY MGO
GEARS WORKING: ABT 6 MT/DAY IFO(380CST) + ABT 0.1 MT/DAY MGO
VLSFO 380 SPEC: RMG 380 WITH MAX SULPHUR0.5% COMPLYING INTERNATIONAL STANDARD ISO 8217 2017 OR 2010 IF 2017 NOT AVAILABLE
LSMGO SPEC: DMA/MAX SULPHUR 0.1% ISO 8217 2017 OR 2010 IF 2017 NOT AVAILABLE
ALL BUNKERS SUPPLIED TO BE FREE OF ANY AND ALL WASTE MARINE OR AUTOMOTIVE LUBRICANTS OR WASTE CHEMICALS.
NO CO-MINGLING OF DIFFERENT BUNKER GRADES OR STEMS.
ANY SAVING IN CONSUMPTION MUST BE OFF-SET AGAINST ANY REDUCTION IN SPEED, ANY SAVING IN TIME MUST BE OFF-SET AGAINST ANY EXCESS CONSUMPTION, ANY SAVING IN FO MUST BE OFF-SET AGAINST INCREASED MGO AND VICE VERSA,
AND ANY OVERALL SAVING ON INDIVIDUAL PASSAGE(S) MUST BE SET OFF AGAINST AN OVERALL LOSS ON OTHER INDIVIDUAL PASSAGE(S).
COST AND TIME (INCLUDING ANY DEVIATION TIME REQUIRED TO MEET SECA REQUIREMENTS AND/OR NATIONAL REGULATIONS IN OPERATION IN PORTS TO WHICH VESSEL IS BOUND) INCL BALLAST EXCHANGE TO BE FOR CHARTERERS\' ACCOUNT.
UNDER NO CIRCUMSTANCES WILL ANY CLAIM BE DEDUCTED FROM HIRE UNLESS AND UNTIL IT HAS BEEN AGREED BY BOTH PARTIES.
VESSEL HAS LIBERTY TO CONSUME MGO WHEN MANEUVERING, IN/OUT OF PORTS, STARTING AUXILIARY ENGINE, NAVIGATION IN SHALLOW/RESTRICTED/CONGESTION/POOR VISIBILITY, CANAL, STRAITS AND RIVERS.
IN THE EVENT THAT CHARTERERS ORDER THE VESSEL TO PORT(S)/PLACES/RIVERS FOR WHERE THE VESSEL STAY IS PROLONGED AN AGGREGATED PERIOD EXCEEDING 20 DAYS ACCORDING TO CHARTERERS\' ORDERS/INSTRUCTIONS,
CHARTERERS HAVE THE OBLIGATION TO TIMELY ARRANGE INSPECTION AND CLEANING OF THE HULL AND UNDERWATER PARTS BUT ALWAYS BEFORE REDELIVERY OF THE VESSEL .
THE SPEED AND CONSUMPTION WARRANTY SHALL BE SUSPENDED ONLY UNTIL THE HULL AND PROPELLER HAVE BEEN CLEANED TO THE SATISFACTION OF MASTER AND OWNERS.

SPEED AND CONSUMPTION BASIS GOOD WEATHER CONDITIONS OF UP TO BEAUFORT FORCE 4 AND DOUGLAS SEA STATE 3 (SIGNIFICANT WAVE HEIGHT 1.25M) WITH NO ADVERSE CURRENTS AND NO NEGATIVE INFLUENCE OF SWELL AND NO ABNORMAL CONDITIONS AFFECTING IN 24 CONSECUTIVE HOURS.
VESSEL’S PERFORMANCE SHOULD BE ASSESSED/CONSIDERED ON THE BASIS OF AVERAGE SPEED AND CONSUMPTION ON SEA PASSAGES FROM SEA BUOY TO SEA BUOY EXCLUDING ANY VOYAGE LESS THAN 48 HOURS DURATION, AND PERIODS DURING WHICH REDUCTIONS OF SPEED FOR SAFETY, REDUCED VISIBILITY, CANALS, NARROW/RESTRICTED WATERS AND ALWAYS IN GOOD WEATHER CONDITIONS.
ABOUT MEANS 0.5 KNOT ALLOWANCE ON SPEED AND 5% ALLOWANCE ON BUNKER CONSUMPTION.  BUNKER SAVING TO OFFSET ANY TIME LOSS AND VICE VERSA FOR EACH VOYAGE (BALLAST AND LADEN).
